parts: Lagoseris frigida is a plant species commonly found in the Andean region of South America. intro: Lagoseris frigida, also known as the cold plant, is a type of succulent plant native to the Andes mountains in South America, characterized by its unique ability to withstand extreme cold temperatures. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, making it useful in treating various health issues such as wounds, skin irritations, and respiratory infections. The bioactive constituents of Lagoseris frigida include flavonoids, alkaloids, and glycosides, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Lagoseris frigida is typically used to make teas, infusions, and ointments, which are commonly used to treat colds, coughs, and skin conditions.
